summary.summary: Removes explicit allowsConstrainedNetworkAccess = false assignments from all Nightscout upload URLRequest constructions in Trio/Sources/Services/Network/Nightscout/NightscoutAPI.swift (diff:f2c6dbfcae37, file:d9d6cc91cab4, commit:cd00ea5bdd73). The change is described in the PR text (pr:nightscout/Trio#1309, pr:gordolio/Trio#1309).
summary.userFacingImpact: The change restores the default URLRequest behavior so iOS decides how to handle constrained network access according to the user’s system settings (pr:gordolio/Trio#1309, pr:nightscout/Trio#1309). The PR was motivated by observed intermittent Nightscout upload errors containing the message “Constrained path prohibited” (pr:gordolio/Trio#1309, pr:nightscout/Trio#1309). The patch itself only removes the explicit flag; it does not include testing or runtime telemetry demonstrating improved reliability, so human review and testing are recommended to validate upload behavior under constrained networks (diff:f2c6dbfcae37, commit:cd00ea5bdd73).
